dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4119 IS: Student Loan Marriage Penalty Elimination Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Warnock (for himself, Mr. Lankford, Ms. Lummis, and Mr. Bennet) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to allow married couples to apply the student loan interest deduction limitation separately to each spouse, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Student Loan Marriage Penalty Elimination Act of 2026.

section.header: Student loan interest deduction limitation applied separately to each spouse

subsection.text: Section 221(b)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended to read as follows:

paragraph.text: The interest taken into account with respect to a taxpayer for a taxable year under subsection (a) for indebtedness incurred by an individual shall not exceed $2,500.

subsection.text: Section 221 of such Code is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (b), by striking the heading and inserting Dollar limitations, and
by amending subsection (e) to read as follows:

subsection.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this section for any amount for which a deduction is allowable under any other provision of this chapter.
The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2026.
